調解案集调解案集Mediation Casebook
調解案集 · 內容均為虛構，僅供調解員練習之用 调解案集 · 内容均为虚构，仅供调解员练习之用 Mediation Casebook · All content fictional, for mediator practice only

案例等級標準 案例等级标准 Case Level Standard

L1 / L2 / L3 / L4 為學員準備度之分層，並非任何考試之等級對應。 L1 / L2 / L3 / L4 为学员准备度之分层，并非任何考试之等级对应。 L1 / L2 / L3 / L4 are trainee-readiness tiers, not an equivalence to any examination grade.

維度Level 1Level 2Level 3Level 4
爭議事項一項主要爭議，可附帶一兩項輕微附屬事項兩至四項相互糾纏之事項，一項之解決會影響另一項五項或以上事項相互交織；其中至少一項為結構性或關係性問題，無法單以金錢解決多項事項加上一個無法於調解中完全解決之公益或監管維度；部分爭議涉及第三方權益
金錢複雜度單一金額或簡單扣減；算術直觀多項分項、分期支付、或結構性和解；算術須細心處理多軌金額：修訂條款、有條件支付、級距上調、交叉抵押或分階段注資多軌金額：分階段支付、信託安排、條件觸發、按場外結果調整；金額取決於桌外發展
文件狀況雙方均有清晰書面紀錄；無事實空隙有部分書面紀錄，但關鍵事實依賴語音訊息、口頭討論或條款解讀文件繁多；條款解讀有爭議；常需專家報告；部分事實有所爭議海量文件加上專家報告；部分事實受保密令或調查限制，當事人不能完全披露
場外角色並無活躍場外角色；最多一名旁觀者並非爭議推動者一至兩名活躍場外角色（如介紹人、知悉中之監管機關）三名或以上活躍場外角色（銀團成員、監管機關、家族共同擔保人、審計師），其同意或立場直接影響結果雖然調解桌上仍只有雙方，但多個場外主體（監管機關、受獨立紀律程序之第三方、少數股東等）並行運作，各有獨立程序及異議權，其立場直接限制和解空間
時間壓力寬鬆；雙方可自行選擇解決時間有明確期限（如通知期、學期），但可延長有外部硬性期限（法定窗口、補救期、財政年度截止）超出雙方控制多重硬性期限相互交錯（披露窗口、聆訊日期、調查時限），且部分由場外主體控制
權力不對稱單軸，輕微（如小商戶對消費者）雙軸（如企業對個人＋專業聲譽涉及其中）多軸（機構對個人＋專業紀錄＋家族／社區地位＋跨境管轄）多軸且動態：機構對個人、專業紀錄、跨境、公益對私利；當事人之 BATNA 可能於會議中途改變
正當性框架雙方各自有一主導框架（通常為合約或公平）兩種框架相互張力（合約對慣例；履行對衡平）三種或以上框架同時運作，包括行業慣例、監管期望、專業紀錄保護多重框架同時運作並相互衝突，包括公益、監管期望、刑事責任邊界及第三方權益保護
調解員技能要求主動聆聽、重新框架、基本選項產生私下會議管理、現實檢驗、措辭精細擬定、處理情緒浮面多方協調、專家證據處理、離場管理、複雜和解結構、平行軌道設計共同調解協調、資訊次序管理、會議中途揭示之判斷、管理桌外平行程序對桌上談判之影響、可承受多重監管審視之措辭設計
離場風險低；雙方均欲達成解決中度；若處理不當，因尊嚴問題可能情緒反轉高；若專業紀錄、家族地位或機構立場被公開定性，任何一方均可能離場極高；任何一方離場均會即時觸發桌外程序（調查、訴訟、紀律審查），令離場之代價遠高於一般個案
和解結構單一文件，即時或短期執行含條件之和解，附明確履行窗口多文件和解：旁註書、延後條件、第三方同意、持續履行檢視多文件和解，部分條款取決於桌外主體之同意；和解本身可能無法完全約束所有受影響之第三方
聯合摘要目標字數700 至 800 字800 至 1,000 字900 至 1,100 字1,300 至 1,800 字
培訓階段學員建立核心調解技能；入門練習已有基礎技能之學員，朝向中段培訓之準備進階或考取資格後之練習；僅供經驗豐富之學員為已掌握 L3 之學員提供之加難挑戰；給希望於同一考試中挑戰更高難度個案之較強學員作準備，並非考取資格後之階段
维度Level 1Level 2Level 3Level 4
争议事项一项主要争议，可附带一两项轻微附属事项两至四项相互纠缠之事项，一项之解决会影响另一项五项或以上事项相互交织；其中至少一项为结构性或关系性问题，无法单以金钱解决多项事项加上一个无法于调解中完全解决之公益或监管维度；部分争议涉及第三方权益
金钱复杂度单一金额或简单扣减；算术直观多项分项、分期支付、或结构性和解；算术须细心处理多轨金额：修订条款、有条件支付、级距上调、交叉抵押或分阶段注资多轨金额：分阶段支付、信托安排、条件触发、按场外结果调整；金额取决于桌外发展
文件状况双方均有清晰书面纪录；无事实空隙有部分书面纪录，但关键事实依赖语音讯息、口头讨论或条款解读文件繁多；条款解读有争议；常需专家报告；部分事实有所争议海量文件加上专家报告；部分事实受保密令或调查限制，当事人不能完全披露
场外角色并无活跃场外角色；最多一名旁观者并非争议推动者一至两名活跃场外角色（如介绍人、知悉中之监管机关）三名或以上活跃场外角色（银团成员、监管机关、家族共同担保人、审计师），其同意或立场直接影响结果虽然调解桌上仍只有双方，但多个场外主体（监管机关、受独立纪律程序之第三方、少数股东等）并行运作，各有独立程序及异议权，其立场直接限制和解空间
时间压力宽松；双方可自行选择解决时间有明确期限（如通知期、学期），但可延长有外部硬性期限（法定窗口、补救期、财政年度截止）超出双方控制多重硬性期限相互交错（披露窗口、听证日期、调查时限），且部分由场外主体控制
权力不对称单轴，轻微（如小商户对消费者）双轴（如企业对个人＋专业声誉涉及其中）多轴（机构对个人＋专业纪录＋家族／社区地位＋跨境管辖）多轴且动态：机构对个人、专业纪录、跨境、公益对私利；当事人之 BATNA 可能于会议中途改变
正当性框架双方各自有一主导框架（通常为合约或公平）两种框架相互张力（合约对惯例；履行对衡平）三种或以上框架同时运作，包括行业惯例、监管期望、专业纪录保护多重框架同时运作并相互冲突，包括公益、监管期望、刑事责任边界及第三方权益保护
调解员技能要求主动聆听、重新框架、基本选项产生私下会议管理、现实检验、措辞精细拟定、处理情绪浮面多方协调、专家证据处理、离场管理、复杂和解结构、平行轨道设计共同调解协调、资讯次序管理、会议中途揭示之判断、管理桌外平行程序对桌上谈判之影响、可承受多重监管审视之措辞设计
离场风险低；双方均欲达成解决中度；若处理不当，因尊严问题可能情绪反转高；若专业纪录、家族地位或机构立场被公开定性，任何一方均可能离场极高；任何一方离场均会即时触发桌外程序（调查、诉讼、纪律审查），令离场之代价远高于一般个案
和解结构单一文件，即时或短期执行含条件之和解，附明确履行窗口多文件和解：旁注书、延后条件、第三方同意、持续履行检视多文件和解，部分条款取决于桌外主体之同意；和解本身可能无法完全约束所有受影响之第三方
联合摘要目标字数700 至 800 字800 至 1,000 字900 至 1,100 字1,300 至 1,800 字
培训阶段学员建立核心调解技能；入门练习已有基础技能之学员，朝向中段培训之准备进阶或考取资格后之练习；仅供经验丰富之学员为已掌握 L3 之学员提供之加难挑战；给希望于同一考试中挑战更高难度个案之较强学员作准备，并非考取资格后之阶段
DimensionLevel 1Level 2Level 3Level 4
Issues in disputeOne main issue, optionally with one or two minor subsidiary itemsTwo to four entangled issues, where movement on one affects anotherFive or more issues bound together; at least one is a structural or relational question that cannot be resolved by money aloneMultiple issues plus a public-interest or regulatory dimension that mediation cannot fully resolve; some matters touch third-party rights
Money complexitySingle sum or simple deduction; arithmetic is obviousMultiple line items, partial payments, or a structured settlement; arithmetic requires careMulti-track quantum: revised terms, conditional payments, step-ups, cross-collateralisation, or staged injectionsMulti-track quantum: staged payments, trust arrangements, conditional triggers, amounts adjusted by off-stage outcomes; the figure turns on developments away from the table
Documentary stateClean written record on both sides; no factual gapsSome written record but key facts rest on voice messages, oral discussions, or clause interpretationHeavy documentary record; disputed clauses; expert reports often required; some facts contestedVoluminous documents plus expert reports; some facts are constrained by confidentiality orders or investigations and cannot be fully disclosed
Off-stage actorsNone active; one bystander at most who does not drive the disputeOne to two active off-stage parties (e.g. a referrer, a regulator on notice)Three or more active off-stage parties (syndicate members, regulators, family co-signers, audit firm) whose consent or position shapes the outcomeStill only two parties at the table, but several off-stage actors (regulators, a third party under separate disciplinary process, minority shareholders) run in parallel, each with independent process and dissent rights that directly constrain the settlement space
Time pressureSoft; parties choose when to resolveDefined deadline (e.g. notice period, school term) but extendableHard external deadline (statutory window, cure period, financial-year close) outside the parties' controlMultiple hard deadlines interlocking (disclosure windows, hearing dates, investigation limits), some controlled by off-stage actors
Power asymmetrySingle axis, mild (e.g. small business vs consumer)Two axes (e.g. corporate vs individual plus professional reputation at stake)Multi-axis (institutional vs individual plus professional record plus family or community standing plus cross-jurisdiction)Multi-axis and dynamic: institutional vs individual, professional record, cross-border, public vs private interest; a party’s BATNA may shift mid-session
Legitimacy framingsOne framing dominates each side (usually contract or fairness)Two framings in tension (contract vs custom; performance vs equity)Three or more framings in play, including industry norms, regulatory expectation, professional-record protectionMultiple framings operating and conflicting at once, including public interest, regulatory expectation, criminal-liability boundaries, and third-party protection
Mediator skill demandActive listening, reframing, basic option-generationCaucus management, reality-testing, drafting nuanced wording, handling emotional surfacesMulti-party coordination, expert-evidence handling, walk-out management, complex settlement structuring, parallel-track designCo-mediation coordination, information-sequencing, judgement on mid-session revelations, managing the bearing of parallel off-stage process on the table negotiation, wording that withstands multiple layers of regulatory scrutiny
Walk-out riskLow; parties want resolutionMedium; emotional reversals possible if dignity issues mishandledHigh; either side may walk if professional record, family standing, or institutional position is publicly framedVery high; a walk-out by either party immediately triggers off-stage process (investigation, litigation, disciplinary review), making the cost of leaving far higher than in an ordinary case
Settlement structureSingle document, immediate or short-period executionSettlement with conditions and a defined performance windowMulti-document settlement: side letters, deferred conditions, third-party consents, ongoing performance reviewsMulti-document settlement, some terms contingent on off-stage consent; the settlement itself may not fully bind all affected third parties
Joint brief target length700 to 800 characters (Chinese)800 to 1,000 characters900 to 1,100 characters1,300 to 1,800 characters
Training stageTrainees building core mediation skills; introductory practiceTrainees with foundational skills, building toward intermediate readinessAdvanced or post-qualification practice; experienced trainees onlyA harder challenge for students who have mastered L3; preparation for a stronger student who wishes to attempt a more demanding case at the same examination, not a post-qualification stage

本案集之 L1 / L2 / L3 / L4 為學員準備度之分層，並非任何考試或專業資格之等級對應。本案集所有內容均屬虛構，僅供調解員練習之用。 本案集之 L1 / L2 / L3 / L4 为学员准备度之分层，并非任何考试或专业资格之等级对应。本案集所有内容均属虚构，仅供调解员练习之用。 The L1 / L2 / L3 / L4 framework in this casebook is a trainee-readiness tiering, not an equivalence to any examination or professional accreditation grade. All content in this casebook is fictional, for mediator practice only.
